title: Model Serving Strategies Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of real-time vs batch inference, serving frameworks, and production deployment patterns.

questions.question: A fraud detection system must block a transaction before it completes. Which inference mode is appropriate?

options.text: Batch inference — schedule it to run nightly
Real-time inference — respond within milliseconds to the API call
Streaming inference — publish to Kafka and consume later
Shadow mode — run both and compare results offline

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of batch inference over real-time inference?

options.text: Lower latency for the end user
It can run on much larger datasets at lower cost per prediction
It requires less memory
It is more accurate than real-time models

questions.question: What does ONNX (Open Neural Network Exchange) primarily enable?

options.text: Training models faster using distributed computing
Converting models from any framework into a unified format runnable by optimized runtimes
Storing model weights in a compressed database
Managing model experiments with version control

questions.question: NVIDIA's Triton Inference Server uses 'dynamic batching'. What problem does this solve?

options.text: It trains a new model version every time a request arrives
It groups multiple incoming individual requests together to maximize GPU utilization
It caches identical requests in Redis to avoid hitting the model
It splits a single large model across multiple GPUs

questions.question: You deploy a new model version but want to validate it on real traffic before fully switching over. What strategy should you use?

options.text: Immediately replace the old model with the new one and monitor for complaints
Only test the new model on synthetic data, then deploy
Use shadow mode — send a copy of live traffic to the new model without exposing its results to users
Disable the old endpoint and ask users to use the new one manually
